user_message: I need to build the narrative for the Evergreen Solutions engagement. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 10:46 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions account health

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 10:43 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 14:50 EST
Subject: account health - Action Required

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-03-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:12:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:27:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-H trap (Skips 'open items from last meeting'); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-H failure mode (Skips 'open items from last meeting')
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-H -- Skips 'open items from last meeting'
